question: Implement a `MyString` class that dynamically allocates memory for a character array (C-style string). Provide a constructor that takes a `const char*` to initialize the string. Crucially, implement a copy constructor to perform a deep copy, preventing issues like double-freeing or shared pointers when `MyString` objects are copied.

test_cases.input: Various MyString operations: construction from C-string, copy construction, copy assignment, empty string, nullptr input, pass-by-value to function, and self-assignment.
test_cases.expected:
Testing MyString - Correct Version.
s1: Hello, World! (length: 13)
s2 (copy of s1): Hello, World! (length: 13)
s1 (modified): Modified s1 (length: 11)
s2 (should be unchanged): Hello, World! (length: 13)
s3 (initial): Initial s3 value (length: 16)
s3 (assigned s2): Hello, World! (length: 13)
s4 (empty):  (length: 0)
s5 (nullptr input):  (length: 0)

Calling printAndCopy with s1:
  Inside printAndCopy function:
    Copied string: Modified s1 (length: 11)
Back in main. s1 remains: Modified s1 (length: 11)

Testing self-assignment (s6 = s6):
s6 before self-assign: Self-assign test
s6 after self-assign: Self-assign test

MyString objects will now go out of scope and destruct.
